epigraph.quote: It turned out to be brandy. By the time Eve had drunk Charles's + Thomas's + her own, she was in a much better mood.

moments.quote: The ferry left at 8 pm & got in at 7am on Thu. It was rather sickening.
The town had lovely balconies & a market where we bought food. John & Charles went in the playground.
The tide was coming in & covering up John's & Charles' holes in the sand.
Simon & Eve walked past a field with cows in down to the "prettiest village in Spain." It is beautiful in all directions.

images.caption: St. Juliana taking the demon captive

moments.place: San Vicente de la Barquera, Cantabria, Spain
moments.quote: we tasted cheeses + sausage at the market. The town was built on a good defensive position on 2 estuaries.
The church was firmly shut for the service, + Simon was wearing shorts, so we didn't attempt to go. It's enough to make you turn Quaker.

images.caption: Peter + John, Thomas + Charles in boats.

moments.quote: We stopped to have tea at Riano which is on a mega lake, a small marina + modern houses.

images.caption: Looking East across the Picos after crossing the pass at Ponton

moments.place: Mansilla de las Mulas, León, Spain
moments.quote: Most villages have a stork's nest on the church.
Grills around monuments - Charles's question to me "Mummy, why don't they let you see Jesus?"

moments.place: San Martín de Castañeda, Zamora, Spain
moments.quote: The 4 boys played in the river morning and afternoon.
OCebreiro is high up, with lovely views of hills, on the Pilgrimage route. Some of the thatched rooves with plaited ropes over them.
The valley was flooded for controlling water for irrigation, + the church was brought up, stone by stone, to a higher village. The stones still had numbers on.
tourists swarm all around, admiring, gawping, eating crepes, achieving their goal.
We had a delicious lunch - 2 crabs among 3 of us
We crossed into Portugal, after Eve drove a lightening tour in Vigo.
a glorious view, first of the sunset across the city, then of skyscrapers sparking in dawn sun - churches, working cranes.
We visited St. Francis Church. Amazeing. Covered in a gold fungus.
The campsite smells of beautiful pines.
Amazing view of ridges of mountains to the south. Very tiring zig zag roads.
3 people were filling up bottles at the 2 village taps. 3 churches, 2 locked & one open – roof has fallen in.
we arrived at Ciudad Rodrigo, & saw the cathedral with good Romanesque carvings. The square was pretty, people dancing and celebrating, we had icecream.
It had a pale turquoise swimming pool + lovely views of hills. We swam. We picked blackberries.

images.caption: Bodegas Dueñas Tinto Puente wine label

moments.quote: We had the best meal of the trip at San Ricardo, inside pink granite portals.
The storm came close so the boys weren't allowed to swim at first. Then we all swam.
We next stopped in a field of poplars near Lerma. Fewer wasps.
The village was pretty, a monastery is there, but we drove on.

moments.place: Santo Domingo de Silos, Burgos, Spain
moments.quote: a beautiful cloister, but the queue was too long.
a lovely clover-shaped swimming pool + a canopy of pines.
Wash house, long + low, with river, mill-race, flowing through. Stones for women to kneel + rub clothes.
The best campsite we've been in this holiday - We are in a circular courtyard.
John + Charles + I joined in some dancing.
We set off from Portsmouth for Birmingham at 4.40. pm.
